items.def: Running a structure to waste the Killer's time while conserving pallets.
Old mechanic where dropping a pallet teleported Survivors through it; removed to make pallets riskier.
Safe position at a loop to watch the Killer's red stain without committing.
Abandoning a loop to run straight to the next tile for distance.
Choosing not to drop a pallet and risking a hit to save resources.
Dropping a pallet early for guaranteed safety but poor efficiency.
Tight spin to break auto-aim during a lunge.
Abusing the Killer's limited vision by moving into their blind spot up close.
Passing through the Killer's collision while they vault a window to lose them.
Vaulting a pallet as the Killer breaks it to force a pickup prompt and set up a blind.
Vaulting back into the Killer immediately after a pallet stun to bait a swing.
Sudden direction change to bait a Killer miss.
Using a locker to dodge/deny a hit or force a weird interaction.
Killer actively pursuing a Survivor.
A map 'piece' (pallet+window layout) that supports a loop.
Pretend to leave a loop, then immediately return.
Decide to stick with a chase or abandon it.
Killer pauses expecting pallet drop; survivor doesn't drop.
Killer takes the stun to maintain pressure.
Kick/destroy a dropped pallet.
Speed boost killer gains in extended chase.
The red light in front of killer; used in mindgames.
Walk backwards to hide red stain while approaching.
Line of sight.
Universal landmark with a strong window and God Pallet.
Central unique structure with strong windows or loops.
Complex wall tiles like T-L, 4-Lane, Long Wall with windows/pallets.
Maze tile shaped like a T and L; two windows, no pallet.
Parallel long walls with a pallet; vision-blocking maze tile.
Coldwind landmark with a safe circular loop around a hanging carcass.
Pallet that guarantees safety; Killer must break it.
Pallet that can be mind-gamed or hit through; offers little safety.
Generic small loops filling space between landmarks; RNG quality.
Corner with no hooks/resources; used to deny hooks.
Area where pallets/walls are gone, making chases unwinnable for Survivors.
House variant with an extremely strong loop/basement setup.
Three close generators controlled by the Killer to stall the match.
Generator; the main survivor objective.
Killer's ability to stop gens getting done.
Survivors intentionally complete gens far apart to avoid a 3-gen.
Touching a gen briefly to stop regression or check progress.
Bring a gen to ~99% and leave it to pop later for timing.
Finish a generator; 'pop this gen'.
Gens losing progress from kick or perk effects.
Killer damaging a gen to start regression and trigger perks.
Sliding along collision during a dash to turn tightly instead of bumping.
Wesker dash manipulation to curve or reverse direction.
Rapid camera turn at dash end to hit around corners.
Hillbilly chainsaw drift using early high turn rate to round walls.
Choosing bump targets to set up the next Blight rush angle.
Feigning direction (red stain, moonwalk) to trick Survivors into a hit.
Forcing Survivor movement with power/threat to drive them into danger.
Guarding a hooked Survivor from nearby positions to intercept rescues.
Leaving Survivors in dying state to apply pressure or deny resets.
Focusing one Survivor to remove them early and create a 3v1.
Staying near hook to secure a kill or deny rescue.
Standing right in front of hook; most extreme form of camping.
Go straight back to the unhooked survivor ASAP.
Prioritise the unhooked, but hit/hook others if convenient.
Leave someone downed while hunting last survivor/hatch.
One down leads into multiple downs quickly; momentum swing.
Wraith nickname from his bell sound.
Cannibal nickname from Leatherface's name.
Hillbilly nickname; 'Space' references old flight glitches.
The Shape nickname using his real name.
The Doctor nickname.
Executioner nickname from helmet shape.
Mastermind nickname play on his name.
Nemesis nicknames from pop culture.
The Demogorgon nickname.
The Pig; often paired with 'boop the snoot' meme.
Onryo nickname based on pronunciation.
The Unknown nickname referencing model anatomy.
Prioritizing rapid generator completion over other actions.
Stealthy Claudette hiding in bushes instead of doing objectives.
Inexperienced player, often a default Dwight.
Stealth-heavy playstyle avoiding chases.
Survive With Friends; coordinated premade team.
Solo queue with random teammates and limited info.
SWF focused on harassing the Killer with flashlights/sabo.
Team dedicated to sabotaging hooks to deny carries.
Instant unhooks in front of the Killer, often unsafe.
Hex: No One Escapes Death; insta-down and speed when gates are powered.
Decisive Strike; escape Killer's grasp after unhook.
Dead Hard; dash/endurance to avoid damage after safe unhook.
Borrowed Time; extends endurance for unhooked Survivors.
Sprint Burst; speed boost when starting to run.
Adrenaline; heal one state and sprint when gates are powered.
Unbreakable; recover from dying state once per trial.
Off the Record; endurance and scratch mark hiding after unhook.
Prove Thyself; repair speed bonus when working with others.
Pop Goes the Weasel; hook-triggered gen regression kick.
Scourge Hook: Pain Resonance; explodes most progressed gen on scourge hook.
Corrupt Intervention; blocks 3 furthest gens at trial start.
Windows of Opportunity; shows pallets/windows.
For the People; trade a health state to instantly heal a teammate.
Synergy perk historically paired with FTP for endurance pickups.
Barbecue & Chilli; aura read after hooking (formerly bonus BP).
Good game; standard post-match courtesy.
Good luck, have fun; pre-match greeting.
Bad manners; teabagging, hitting on hook, flashlight clicking, etc.
Rapid crouching to taunt or say hello.
Teammate accidentally or intentionally gets you hit/caught.
Playing very seriously/optimally; usually said negatively.
Twitch streamer tag in name; often attracts extra attention.
Usually sarcasm/salt in endgame chat.
Friendly Pig interaction; crouch/point to request mercy.
Slow meme build using tombstone/vanity mirror add-ons.
Jumpscare build with wall-hack add-on, Tier 1 terror radius.
Flashlight spam to taunt or demand aggro.
Killer hitting a hooked Survivor; seen as BM.
Killer is chasing me.
Killer left chase.
Killer staying near hook.
Keep doing the gen.
Disengage; don't greed the rescue/gen.
I'll take hook so you can get out / reset.
Everyone heal and spread.
Iridescent; highest rarity tier (pink/red items and add-ons).
Very Rare rarity tier.
Rare rarity tier.
Uncommon rarity tier.
Common rarity tier (lowest).
Blind killer during pickup animation to drop survivor.
Flashlight or firecracker blind on the Killer.
Stunning killer during pickup to free survivor.
Use your body to block the Killer's path.
Taking a hit meant for someone else.
Sabotaging hooks with toolbox or perks.
Healing someone right at the hook; often debated if safe.
Heal to 99% and finish later for timing.
Rescue someone from hook.
Hook someone again soon after; often said alongside 'tunnel'.
Unlit totem with no active effect.
Killer perk tied to a lit totem; destroyed when cleansed.
Break a hex or dull totem.
Survivor 'blessed' totem creating an aura zone with effects.
Apply a boon perk to a totem.
Killer kills 0–4 survivors; 4K is a clean win, 2K often treated as a draw.
Number of survivors escaping.
Last survivor escapes via hatch.
Survivor escapes via an opened exit gate.
Bloodpoints; in-game currency for progression.
Gaining or losing progress in grade.
Challenge tracks and battle-pass style progression.
Size of the SWF group.
Very coordinated/strong premade group.
Basic health states.
On the ground; can crawl and will bleed out.
Survivor dies from being left downed long enough.
Killer picks a downed survivor up for hook/carry.
Survivor escape attempt while being carried.
Hanging normally on hook.
Entity attempts; requires skill checks.
Next hook kills; you've been hooked twice already.
Endgame Collapse; timer after gates powered and opened or hatch closed.
